status_detail: Final Investment Decision taken July 24–25, 2024. Manufacturing contract signed August 13, 2024 with ITM Power. Stack manufacturing ongoing 2025–2026 at ITM Power's Bessemer Park factory (UK). Linde Engineering serving as EPC integrator. Plant to be located at Shell Energy and Chemicals Park Rhineland in Wesseling, near Cologne. Planned start of operations 2027. Project enabled by EU binding renewable hydrogen targets and German regulatory framework. Received EU Horizon 2020 grant funding. Scale-up of successful 10 MW REFHYNE I plant (operational since 2021 at same site).

data_management.narrative_summary: REFHYNE II is a 100 MW PEM electrolysis project at Shell's Energy and Chemicals Park Rhineland in Wesseling, Germany, near Cologne. It is a scale-up of the successful 10 MW REFHYNE I plant that has been operating at the same site since 2021. The project uses ITM Power TRIDENT PEM stacks and skids, with Linde Engineering serving as the EPC integrator. ITM Power is manufacturing the stacks at its Bessemer Park factory in Sheffield, UK, under a capacity reservation signed in December 2023 and formal contract signed in August 2024. The plant will produce up to 44,000 kg/day (~15,000 tonnes/year) of renewable hydrogen to partially replace grey hydrogen in fuel production at Shell's Wesseling refinery, enabling production of low-carbon fuels meeting EU renewable fuel standards. FID was taken in July 2024 with planned operations starting in 2027. The project benefits from EU Horizon 2020 grant funding and is enabled by the EU's binding renewable hydrogen targets and Germany's regulatory framework.

power.grid_connection_detail: Connected to German grid at Shell Rheinland Energy & Chemicals Park. Renewable electricity procurement per EU RFNBO rules.

offtake.offtaker_name: Shell Rheinland Energy & Chemicals Park (Wesseling refinery) — low-carbon fuel production

rag_metadata.text_for_embedding: REFHYNE II is a 100 MW PEM electrolysis project at Shell Energy and Chemicals Park Rhineland in Wesseling, near Cologne, Germany. Scale-up of successful 10 MW REFHYNE I operating since 2021. Uses ITM Power TRIDENT PEM stacks and skids with Linde Engineering as EPC integrator. Manufacturing at ITM Power's Bessemer Park, Sheffield, UK. Will produce 44,000 kg/day (~15,000 t/yr) renewable hydrogen to partially replace grey hydrogen for low-carbon fuel production meeting EU standards. FID July 2024. Operations planned for 2027. EU Horizon 2020 funded. Under construction. Germany. Europe. refinery + mobility. PEM. 100MW.
